skill.name: skill-builder
skill.description: Guides users through creating Claude Code and Codex agent skills with clear descriptions, file structure, tool restrictions, examples, and optional helper scripts.
skill.summary: Create well-structured agent skills through a guided design and implementation workflow.
skill.author: 0xRaduan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are documentation examples inside markdown templates and security checklists. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious behavior was found. The skill still has medium risk because it guides users to create files, define skill directories, fetch official documentation, and generate runnable helper scripts.

medium_findings.title: Skill Creation Workflow Can Modify Agent Skill Directories
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create all needed files and choose personal or project skill paths. This is legitimate for a skill builder, but it can change assistant behavior by writing to skill directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation workflow and target skill paths are explicit. The risk is contextual because the behavior is expected for this skill, not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Generated Scripts and Dependency Commands Need User Review
medium_findings.description: The skill provides templates and guidance for creating runnable helper scripts and dependency commands. Generated scripts can be useful, but users should review code and packages before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly encourages script creation and shows command usage. The risk is moderate because the package itself does not include executable scripts or force execution.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Fetch Uses a Hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: The skill points to an official Claude documentation URL and suggests fetching it when clarification is needed. This is a bounded documentation lookup, not data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only observed URL is official documentation and no user data is sent. This is a normal reference lookup for a documentation-driven skill.

low_findings.title: Static Eval, Secret, and Hidden-File Hits Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported eval, secret, and hidden-file patterns appear in security review checklists, sample commands, and skill path examples. They are not executable code in the package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside markdown documentation and examples. They do not show actual secret access, eval execution, or unauthorized filesystem access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation Mentions Unsafe eval and Secret Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference guide includes unsafe eval and secret keywords as examples for a security reviewer skill. These are documentation-only indicators and were dismissed as direct threats.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows a checklist telling users what to search for. It is not code that evaluates input or reads credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Templates Include Shell and Install Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell snippets and dependency command examples for generated skills. These commands are instructional templates, but users should review them before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Commands are present in markdown examples and are not automatically executed. The risk depends on the future generated skill content and user approval.

content.value_statement: Creating useful agent skills requires clear triggers, safe structure, and practical examples. This skill guides users from idea to finished skill files.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through defining a skill purpose, trigger scenarios, and target scope.
Helps craft concise skill descriptions that improve model activation.
Recommends file structures for instruction-only, script-powered, and reference-heavy skills.
Provides templates for SKILL.md, optional REFERENCE.md files, and helper scripts.
Explains optional tool restrictions for read-only, file-editing, and unrestricted workflows.
Supports iterative refinement after the user tests whether the skill activates correctly.
limitations: It does not validate generated skills against the latest marketplace policies automatically.
It may suggest scripts, so users must review generated code and dependencies before running them.
It depends on user answers to define scope, tools, and expected behavior accurately.
It references Claude Code conventions and may need adaptation for other agent platforms.

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Skill
use_cases.description: Build a focused skill for repeated personal workflows, such as test review or document processing.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skills
use_cases.description: Design project skills with shared naming, descriptions, examples, and tool restrictions for a repository.

use_cases.title: Prototype Script-Powered Skills
use_cases.description: Plan a skill that includes helper scripts, dependency notes, and clear usage guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for [workflow]. Ask me the key questions and suggest a simple structure.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Activation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill idea and write a better description that includes what it does and when to use it.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Team Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a project skill plan for [team workflow]. Include scope, files, examples, and tool restrictions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Script-Powered Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a skill with helper scripts for [task]. Include dependencies, safety checks, and testing steps.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for reviewing Python test coverage.
output_examples.output: A proposed skill name, activation description, recommended files, dependency notes, and example user prompts for testing activation.

output_examples.input: Make my skill description more specific.
output_examples.output: A tighter description that names the task, trigger words, supported file types, and required tools or packages.

output_examples.input: Should this skill have helper scripts?
output_examples.output: A decision summary explaining when scripts are useful, what files to add, and what safety checks the user should review.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one capability and one clear set of trigger scenarios.
Review generated scripts, package dependencies, and file paths before running or installing anything.
Test the finished skill with realistic prompts and refine the description if activation is unreliable.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that try to handle unrelated workflows.
Do not run generated helper scripts before reviewing their source and dependencies.
Do not omit trigger words, file types, or tool requirements from the skill description.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create agent skills with clear descriptions, structured files, examples, and optional helper scripts.

faq.question: Can it create personal and project skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains both personal skill paths and project skill paths, then adapts the structure to the chosen scope.

faq.question: Does it require scripts?
faq.answer: No. It supports instruction-only skills and suggests scripts only when custom tooling is useful.

faq.question: Can it restrict tool access?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains optional tool restrictions for read-only, file-editing, and unrestricted skill workflows.

faq.question: Is generated code safe to run immediately?
faq.answer: No. Users should review generated scripts, commands, and dependencies before running them.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers, team leads, and automation specialists who want repeatable agent workflows can use it.
